'''Paul Davidson''' (born 1 August 1969) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Hensingham ARLFC]] (in [[Hensingham]], [[Whitehaven]]),<ref name="Hensingham ARLFC at pitchero.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.pitchero.com/clubs/hensinghamarlfc|title=Hensingham ARLFC at pitchero.com|publisher=pitchero.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], the [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham Bears]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]], as a {{rlp|ce|pr}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.

==International honours==
Davidson won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] while at St. Helens as a playing substitute for [[England national rugby league team|Emerging England]] in the 15–12 victory over Wales at [[Halton Stadium|Naughton Park]], [[Widnes]], on Friday 19 June 1998.<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Elder statesmen negotiate England escape|newspaper=The Times|date=20 July 1998|page=32|publisher=News International Trading Limited|location=London|id={{ProQuest|317976921}}}}</ref>
